channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Malinauskas delivers a lesson in unity and messaging amid One Nation’s rise
item.description: The article explores how South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas offered a thoughtful and unifying message in his election victory speech, contrasting with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s more co...

item.title: Police investigate Guelph robberies involving 12-year-old suspect and bear spray
item.description: Guelph police are investigating two recent robberies allegedly involving a 12-year-old boy who reportedly used bear spray during the incidents. According to CTV News, officers were called after separa...

item.title: Central Bank of Nigeria Named Central Bank of the Year 2026 by Central Banking Awards for Economic Stabilisation Reforms
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) has received the prestigious Central Bank of the Year award at the 2026 Central Banking Awards, organised by Central Banking magazine in London. This recognition come...

item.title: Accelerated Coastal Erosion Prompts Concerns About Long-Term Protection for Sizewell C Nuclear Plant
item.description: Recent accelerated coastal erosion along the UK's east coast, exacerbated by a severe winter, has intensified debates about the safeguards for the £40 billion Sizewell C nuclear power station under co...

item.title: Hugo Broos praises Mofokeng and Adams resurgence ahead of Panama friendlies
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has expressed his excitement over the strong return to form of Orlando Pirates attacker Relebohile Mofokeng and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams. As the nationa...

item.title: NIH Researchers Develop Improved Blood Test for Early Detection of Pancreatic Cancer
item.description: Researchers funded by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) have developed a blood test that could transform the early detection of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, one of the deadliest forms of ca...

item.title: HS2 speed reductions considered as part of new cost-saving review
item.description: The HS2 high-speed rail project in the UK is considering a reduction in its planned top speeds as part of a new cost-cutting initiative. Transport Secretary Heidi Alexander has requested HS2 executive...

item.title: African ports see growth as shipping routes bypass Suez Canal
item.description: African bunkering hubs are experiencing notable growth as shipping companies increasingly reroute vessels around the Cape of Good Hope. This shift has been driven by ongoing instability in the Middle ...

item.title: President Marcos inspects Bicol Mega Cold Storage Facility and vows continued support for agricultural infrastructure
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. visited the Bicol Mega Cold Storage and Vegetable and Fruit Processing Facility in Pili, Camarines Sur on March 23, 2026. During his inspection, he promised to continue ...

item.title: Audio Reveals How Air Canada Plane Collided With Vehicle, Killing 2 Pilots
item.description: A tragic accident unfolded at LaGuardia Airport involving an Air Canada Express flight AC-8646 operated by Jazz Aviation. The plane collided with a vehicle on the taxiway as it was trying to land, kil...

item.title: Veteran Sports Journalist S Thyagarajan Passes Away at 85
item.description: Veteran Indian sports journalist S Thyagarajan, widely known for his in-depth coverage of field hockey, passed away in Chennai on Monday at the age of 85 following age-related health issues. He is sur...

item.title: California Residents and Lawmakers Question Health and Energy Impacts of Proposed Data Centers
item.description: A proposed 950,000-square-foot data center in Imperial County, California, has sparked strong opposition from local residents due to concerns about air quality, rising utility costs, and potential hea...

item.title: Understanding Urban Health Challenges and Opportunities in African Cities
item.description: Dis news article talk about how African cities dey grow fast and dey vibrant, but still dey face many health and social challenges. One book, 'Urban Health in Africa', explore how rapid urbanisation a...

item.title: Delhi University Makes Prior Approval Mandatory for All College Events
item.description: Delhi University has introduced stringent guidelines for organising events in its colleges to ensure discipline, transparency, and campus security. The new directive mandates that no event can take pl...

item.title: Minister Makonda Sends Off Dar City Team for Basketball Africa League Tour in South Africa
item.description: The Minister for Culture, Arts, and Sports, Honourable Paul Makonda, has officially flagged off the Dar City basketball team as they depart for South Africa to take part in the prestigious Basketball ...

item.title: Nairobi Dam to Undergo Rehabilitation to Prevent Floods and Protect Environment
item.description: The Nairobi Dam is set for a major rehabilitation after a joint assessment by the County Government of Nairobi, Water Resources Authority, Nairobi Rivers Commission, Nairobi City Water and Sewerage Co...

item.title: Ramaphosa Marks Southern Africa Liberation Day, Highlights Regional Cooperation and Sahrawi Support
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa, in his address on Southern Africa Liberation Day, acknowledged the immense sacrifices made by liberation fighters and ordinary citizens in the struggle against colonialism a...

item.title: EU funds Tanzanian institutions to strengthen training on cross-border disease control
item.description: The European Union (EU) has provided 443,989 Euros to support a health education project in Tanzania aimed at improving the ability of doctors and health workers to identify, control, and manage disea...

item.title: Senator Bong Go provides aid to families affected by Davao fire
item.description: Senator Bong Go paid a visit to families displaced by a fire in Barangay Kapitan Tomas Monteverde, Agdao District, Davao City on Saturday. The fire, which occurred on March 16, left 231 families affec...

item.title: Danone acquires British meal replacement company Huel for €1bn
item.description: Danone has agreed to acquire the British meal supplement company Huel for a reported €1bn (£864m). Founded in Buckinghamshire in 2014, Huel is recognised for its nutritionally complete meal replacemen...
